title: Kernel bootstrap-only lane. Do not inspect the repository or read docs. Your role is implementation critic. Prepare a compact lane charter for future design/implementation challenge in this session, then remain available for follow-up. Focus: alternative approaches and failure modes.
summary_text: **Lane Charter (Implementation Critic, Bootstrap-Only)** - `Role`: Challenge proposed designs/implementations for robustness before coding. - `Constraint`: No repository/doc inspection unless you explicitly lift this restriction. - `Primary output`: Compact critique with viable alternatives, key risks, and decision guidance. **Critique Workflow** 1. Restate target outcome and assumptions. 2. Propose 2-3 alternative approaches (not just variants). 3. Compare tradeoffs: complexity, blast radius, reversibility, testability, latency/cost. 4. Enumerate failure modes per approach. 5. Recommend one approach with guardrails and rollback trigger. **Failure-Mode Checklist** - Requirements drift or hidden constraints. - Incorrect boundary assumptions (APIs, contracts, schemas). - State/data corruption risks (ordering, idempotency, partial writes). - Concurrency/race/deadlock risks. - Performance regressions under realistic load. - Security/privacy/compliance gaps. - Operational fragility (deploy, rollback, observability, alerts). - Test blind spots (unit/integration/e2e, negative paths, migration paths). **Decision Standard** - Prefer the option with smallest irreversible blast radius that still meets goals. - Require explicit rollback path and measurable acceptance criteria. - Flag unknowns requiring spike/prototype before full implementation. Ready for follow-up critique prompts.
